progression_rule: Advance weight when all working sets are completed at the prescribed reps at RPE <= target. Add 2.5kg upper body / 5kg lower body. If RPE exceeds target on last set, hold weight for next session.

weekly_schedule.tuesday: Upper Pull (morning gym — legs spared for evening futsal)
weekly_schedule.thursday: Core + Conditioning (morning gym — legs spared for evening futsal)

metabolic_primer.name: Push-Up to Shoulder Tap
metabolic_primer.instructions: Perform a standard push-up, then at the top tap each shoulder alternately. Maintain a rigid plank position throughout. Scale by dropping to knees if form breaks.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.note: Primary chest strength movement. Tuck elbows ~45 degrees. Full ROM — bar touches chest each rep.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.note: Bench at 30-45 degrees. Emphasizes upper chest. Neutral grip option if shoulder discomfort.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Medial deltoid isolation. Slight forward lean. Control the eccentric — 3 seconds down. No swinging.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Overhead Press
exercises.note: Seated for stability. Press to full lockout. Keep core braced and lower back neutral — no arch.

exercises.name: Cable Chest Fly
exercises.note: High-to-low or mid cable. Squeeze pecs at peak contraction. Slight elbow bend maintained throughout.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.note: Cable or EZ bar. Elbows point to ceiling, stationary. Stretches long head of tricep. Full extension at bottom.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
exercises.note: Straight bar or rope attachment. Elbows pinned to sides. Squeeze at full extension. Finish the session here.

sessions.focus: Upper Pull — Back / Biceps (legs spared — futsal tonight)
sessions.scheduling_note: Morning gym session. Futsal is in the evening. No lower body work — preserve leg freshness for the pitch.

metabolic_primer.name: Band Pull-Aparts + Inverted Rows
metabolic_primer.protocol: Alternate: 15 band pull-aparts → max-rep inverted rows. Repeat for 5 minutes.
metabolic_primer.instructions: Band pull-aparts: hold band at shoulder width, pull apart to full arm extension, control return. Inverted rows: use a barbell in a rack or TRX; body straight, pull chest to bar.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.note: Overhand grip. Torso ~45 degrees. Pull bar to lower sternum. Squeeze scapulae at top. No lower back rounding.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.note: Wide overhand grip. Pull to upper chest. Initiate with lats, not biceps. Controlled return to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.note: Close-grip handle. Sit tall. Pull handle to lower abs. Hold 1 second at peak contraction. Slow eccentric.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.note: Brace on bench. Pull elbow to hip, not to ceiling. Full stretch at bottom. Avoid rotating the torso.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.note: Rope attachment at face height. Pull to forehead, elbows flare high. Rear delt + external rotation. Key injury prevention for shoulders.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Curl
exercises.note: EZ bar reduces wrist stress. Full ROM — full extension at bottom, full contraction at top. No swinging.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.note: Bench at 45 degrees. Arms hang straight down at start — maximum bicep stretch. Supinate wrist at top. Slow eccentric.

metabolic_primer.name: Squat Jumps
metabolic_primer.instructions: Bodyweight squat to parallel, then explode into a vertical jump. Land soft with knees tracking over toes. Reset immediately. Maximally explosive on every rep.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Barbell or dumbbells. Soft knee bend. Push hips back, feel hamstring stretch. Bar stays close to body. Drive hips forward to stand.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.note: Rear foot elevated on bench. Front foot far enough forward that knee tracks over toes. Dumbbells at sides. High futsal carryover — single-leg stability.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.note: Full ROM — dead hang to chin over bar. Add weight via belt if bodyweight feels easy (RPE < 6). Scale with band assistance if needed.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Push Press
exercises.note: Slight knee dip to initiate, then drive dumbbells overhead. Trains explosive power transfer from lower to upper body. High athletic carryover.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Dumbbell in opposite hand. Hinge on one leg, other leg extends behind for counterbalance. Builds unilateral hamstring strength and ankle stability — key for futsal.

exercises.name: TRX Row
exercises.note: Body at 30-45 degrees. Pull chest to hands, elbows drive back. Adjust difficulty by walking feet forward. Core stays rigid throughout.

exercises.name: Plank to Push-Up
exercises.note: Start in forearm plank. Press up to full push-up position one arm at a time, then lower back down. Minimise hip rotation. Alternate leading arm each rep.

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.note: Cable or band at chest height. Press straight out and hold 2 seconds, resist rotation. Anti-rotation core stability — directly transfers to lateral cutting and kicking in futsal.

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning gym session. Futsal is in the evening. No heavy lower body loading. Core, upper accessory, and conditioning only.

metabolic_primer.name: Mountain Climbers + Med Ball Slams
metabolic_primer.protocol: Alternate: 30s mountain climbers → 10 med ball slams. Repeat for 5 minutes with minimal rest.
metabolic_primer.instructions: Mountain climbers: from push-up position, drive knees to chest alternately at speed. Med ball slams: raise ball overhead, slam down explosively, catch on bounce. Use 6-10kg ball.

exercises.name: Cable Woodchop
exercises.note: High-to-low diagonal pull. Pivot back foot on the pull. Trains rotational power essential for kicking mechanics in futsal. Controlled return.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout

warmup_sets.note: Feel the core brace — do not go to failure

exercises.note: From knees. Roll out until hips want to sag — stop there. Pull back using abs, not hip flexors. Increase range progressively over weeks.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.note: Dead hang from pull-up bar. Raise knees to chest (beginner) or straight legs to parallel (advanced). No swinging — control both up and down.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.note: Lie on back, arms vertical, knees at 90 degrees. Extend opposite arm and leg simultaneously while maintaining lower back pressed to floor. Slow and deliberate — 3 seconds per rep.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
exercises.note: Lie on floor, dumbbells at chest. Press to lockout. Floor limits ROM and reduces shoulder stress — good for high-frequency upper days. Elbows stay in contact with floor between reps.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.note: Close-grip handle. Light to moderate load — focus on scapular retraction and rear delt activation. Upper back health maintenance for the week.

exercises.name: Side Plank Hip Dip
exercises.note: Forearm side plank. Lower hip toward floor and raise back to level. Targets obliques and hip abductors. Builds lateral stability critical for cutting in futsal. Hold top position 1 second each rep.

push_week.label: Friday Push Week — Chest / Shoulders / Triceps (DB-focused variation)

metabolic_primer.name: Push-Up to Shoulder Tap
metabolic_primer.instructions: Same as Monday. Compare quality — you should feel more fluid than Monday given accumulated priming.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.note: Replaces barbell. Greater ROM and unilateral balance demand. Dumbbells touch at top, lower to chest level.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.note: Same as Monday. Compare weight — aim for parity or small improvement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Same as Monday. Add 1-2 reps or 0.5kg if Monday felt easy.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Overhead Press
exercises.note: Same as Monday.

exercises.name: Pec Deck
exercises.note: Replaces Cable Chest Fly. Fixed path reduces stabilizer demand — allows higher pec isolation at end of week when fatigue is higher.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.note: Same as Monday.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
exercises.note: Same as Monday. Finish the push session here.

pull_week.label: Friday Pull Week — Back / Biceps (heavier variation)

metabolic_primer.name: Band Pull-Aparts + Inverted Rows
metabolic_primer.protocol: Same as Tuesday. Alternate 15 band pull-aparts → max-rep inverted rows for 5 minutes.

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.note: Replaces BB Row. Landmine setup or T-bar machine. Neutral grip (if available). Similar pattern — heavier load potential. Pull to lower chest.

exercises.name: Weighted Pull-Up
exercises.note: Replaces Lat Pulldown. Add weight via dip belt. If body weight is RPE 8 or above, use bodyweight. Full ROM — dead hang to chin over bar.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.note: Same as Tuesday.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.note: Same as Tuesday.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.note: Same as Tuesday. Mandatory shoulder health work.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Curl
exercises.note: Same as Tuesday.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.note: Same as Tuesday.

metabolic_primer.name: Lateral Bounds
metabolic_primer.instructions: Push off one leg laterally, land on the opposite leg with a soft, controlled landing. Stick the landing for 1 second before bounding back. Maximum horizontal distance per bound. High futsal specificity — mimics defensive slide and lateral sprint mechanics.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.note: Leg day headline movement. High-bar or low-bar depending on preference. Depth to parallel minimum. Brace hard, drive knees out, big chest throughout.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Follow squats with RDL to hit posterior chain while glutes are pre-fatigued. Bar stays close to shins. Feel the hamstring stretch — do not chase heavy weight at the expense of feel.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat

warmup_sets.note: Balance and hip flexor length check

exercises.note: Rear foot elevated. Dumbbells at sides or barbell on back. Front knee tracks over toes. High unilateral carryover to futsal change-of-direction.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.note: Feet mid-platform, shoulder width. Lower until knees reach ~90 degrees. Quad volume accumulation after bilateral work. Knees must not cave inward.

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl

warmup_sets.note: Eccentric hamstring preparation — do not go to failure on warm-up

exercises.note: Anchor feet under a pad or have a partner hold. Lower body toward floor under control — eccentric only. Catch with hands, push back to start. Elite hamstring injury prevention for sprinting athletes. Build volume slowly.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Barbell across hips, upper back on bench. Drive hips to full extension — squeeze glutes hard at top. Pause 1 second. Builds hip extension power critical for sprinting and jumping.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.note: Full ROM — deep stretch at bottom, full plantarflexion at top. Slow 3-second eccentric. Soleus-dominant. Ankle resilience for repeated lateral movement in futsal.

exercises.name: Box Jump

warmup_sets.load: Low box — step down after each rep
warmup_sets.note: CNS activation — not a fatigue set

exercises.note: Use 50-60cm box. Maximal effort each rep — 60-90 seconds full rest between reps. Step down — do not jump down. Final exercise of the session. Trains explosive power and fast-twitch recruitment at the end of volume work.
